title: Rigid Body Dynamics Simulation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Rigid Body Dynamics Simulation: Rigid body dynamics: simulate 6-DOF motion of rigid body under forces and torques using quaternion representation. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.euler.rigid_body_equations…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; inertia_tensor_uncertainty, aerodynamic_drag_neglect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: p_dot = v; q_dot = 0.5*Omega(omega)*q; J*omega_dot = tau - omega x (J*omega); m*v_dot = F
E.observation_y: applied forces F and torques tau

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 6DOF_state_trajectory is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); inertia_tensor_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: attitude error < 0.01 deg for RK4 with dt < 0.001s. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |inertia_tensor_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_timesteps) for Euler equations integration per iteration
